name: Yoghurt Sourdough Starter
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the milk to 100°F on a thermometer.
2. Remove from heat and stir in the yoghurt.
3. Pour into a scalded glass jar or bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and place in a warm location for 18 hours. The consistency will be like thin yoghurt.
4. Stir in the flour until well blended, then cover again with plastic wrap and pierce with a fork to release gases.
5. Place in a warm, draft-free location at an even temperature of 85°F for 2 days; stir several times each day.
6. It should have a strong sourdough smell and show bubbles. Refrigerate until ready to use.
7. When replenishing the starter, add lukewarm milk instead of water.
